name: Equal heights
description: The Equal heights pattern arranges content blocks in a horizontal row where each block maintains the same height regardless of the amount of content inside. This creates a visually balanced layout that allows items to be easily compared and scanned.  An optional [CTA block](/sites/construct/cta-block) can appear at the section level, beneath blocks list.
usage:
The Equal heights pattern presents related content in a horizontal layout using [Equal height block](/sites/construct/equal-height-block), ensuring that each item maintains the same height. This creates consistent alignment across the row and supports a balanced, easy-to-scan layout.

Use this pattern to break down information into columns. It is commonly used to present comparable items such as features, benefits, product options, or key resources.

A minimum of three columns is recommended. Equalising the height of each block prevents uneven layouts when content lengths vary while maintaining consistent spacing across the row.

**Best practice:** Keep the content volume similar across columns to maintain visual balance.

### When to use

*   Content consists of comparable items that benefit from side-by-side presentation, such as features, benefits, product options, or key resources
*   Items share a consistent structure and a similar volume of content
*   Users need to scan and compare multiple items quickly

### When not to use

*   Each item requires rich or varied content such as images, videos, code blocks, or lists — use [Divided section](/sites/construct/divided-section)  instead
*   The content covers a single topic with supporting material — use [Basic section](/sites/construct/basic-section)  instead
*   The content consists of paired titles and descriptions in a text-heavy list format — use [Tiered list](/sites/construct/tiered-list)  instead

table.name: Rule
table.description: The rule component indicates the beginning of a new section.

table.name: Heading
table.description: The section heading communicates the primary subject or theme of the section.

table.name: Subheading
table.description: The optional subheading placed above the body. IIt provides additional context or a short explanation that helps clarify the purpose of the section.

table.name: Body
table.description: A description that provides introductory context for the section. It appears at the top of the section and helps users understand the purpose or scope of the content before engaging with the content below.

table.name: Equal height blocks
table.description: [Equal height block](wiki-ref://grid-ymtdOF6n4R/i-9OaD6bj5Qe) is a content unit that ensures consistent vertical alignment for multiple items arranged within a row.

table.name: CTA block
table.description: [CTA block](wiki-ref://grid-ymtdOF6n4R/i-0Z_OLuJDCc) presents one or more related actions that guide the user toward the next step within a section

properties.name: Section rule
properties.description: The rule component indicates the beginning of a new pattern
properties.constraint: Only use the default style for the section-level rule

properties.name: Linked heading
properties.description: The heading may function as a link when clearly indicated.

properties.name: Subheading
properties.description: The subheading provides additional hierarchy beneath the main title
properties.constraint:
h4: up to 100 characters
h5: up to 160 characters

properties.name: Large layout
properties.description: The intended grid layout for the section.

properties.name: Bottom padding
properties.description: The type of padding applied to the bottom of the pattern

properties.name: Linked heading
properties.description: The heading may function as a link when clearly indicated.

properties.name: Body
properties.constraint: Up to 300 characters (The content is recommended to be limited to a single sentence)

properties.name: Image
properties.description: The image at the top of the block.

properties.name: Image ratio large
properties.description: The aspect ratio to apply to item images on large screens.
options:
- square
- 2:3
- 3:2
- 6:9
- cinematic
- auto(to use the image's original aspect ratio)

properties.name: Image ratio medium
properties.description: The aspect ratio to apply to item images on medium screens.
options:
- square
- 2:3
- 3:2
- 6:9
- cinematic
- auto(to use the image's original aspect ratio)

properties.name: Image ratio small
properties.description: The aspect ratio to apply to item images on small screens.
options:
- square
- 2:3
- 3:2
- 6:9
- cinematic
- auto(to use the image's original aspect ratio)

properties.name: Highlighted image
properties.description: Whether to highlight image with the alt background.

properties.name: Primary CTA
properties.description: The CTA used with the positive button style. It indicates a high-priority, positive action and is reserved for hero areas or other key call-to-action sections.

properties.name: Secondary CTA
properties.description: The CTA using the default button style. It is used for most actions that are positive but not primary, and may serve as the main CTA in non-hero sections.

properties.name: Link CTA
properties.description: The text link used for contextual actions or pathways to more information. It uses a chevron (›) to indicate navigation to additional content or pages.
